treatment_information.treatment_response: Recovered well, swelling subsided by the next morning
Recovered well, swelling subsided by the next morning

treatment_timeline.event: Admission to A/E department with acute swelling
Initial treatment with hydrocortisone and Piriton
Referred to haematologists for possible B cell malignancy
Repeat blood samples showed recovery of complement profile
Bone marrow aspirate and trephine samples taken, no evidence of malignancy

patient_outcome.final_status: No recurrence of symptoms since original acute attack
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Monoclonal gammopathy to be followed up on a regular basis
patient_outcome.recovery_timeline: Swelling subsided by the next morning
